items.title: Darwin father reveals harrowing details of wife's shock killing

children.content: Mick Fairfield arrived home after a normal day at work to find his wife dead with stab wounds and his young daughter hiding under a blanket. He waited years for the case to go to trial, but it never did, and now he wants answers.

gallery.altText: A man with a beard and a baseball cap

thumbnail.altText: A man with a beard and a baseball cap

items.title: Woman, 25, drowns at popular swimming hole near Alice Springs

children.content: The woman died despite resuscitation efforts from paramedics, Northern Territory Police say.

image.altText: Ellery Creek Big Hole is seen on a sunny afternoon.

items.title: Coroner says attempted murder-suicide was 'tragic outcome' of coercive control

children.content: The Northern Territory coroner has found an attempted murder-suicide in Darwin was a "tragic outcome" of coercive control.

image.altText: A woman sitting at her desk, with bookshelves in the background, and looking through a thick file of papers.

items.title: NT police no longer calling for information about man found dead near roadway

children.content: Northern Territory police say they are no longer calling for information about a man who was found dead near a roadway in the remote town of Tennant Creek.

image.altText: A Northern Territory police vehicle in Katherine.

items.title: Lying immobile after a remote chopper crash, Fred felt something biting his leg

children.content: In the aftermath of a 2019 helicopter crash in Kakadu National Park, Fred Hunter lay on the ground covered in blood and fuel, when he felt something biting his leg. On the 20th anniversary of CareFlight, Mr Hunter recounts his dramatic rescue by the service.

image.altText: Wide-shot, thigh-up of tan man, white / gray t-shirt, beige shorts, standing among tropical forest trees, looking up at sky

items.title: 'She just lost every coin flip': How a short hike went so wrong for Celine Cremer

children.content: Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer was on the trip of a lifetime, but on a hike in Tasmania she left the trail and was never seen again. It took a private investigator, a YouTuber, loved ones and an army of volunteers to find answers.

gallery.altText: A green background with a composite of two images- a young woman and a slice of satellite map indicating locations

thumbnail.altText: A green background with a composite of two images- a young woman and a slice of satellite map indicating locations

items.title: Underground 3D printing farms arming Ukraine's soldiers

children.content: In spare rooms and backyard sheds, volunteers across Europe are 3D printing drone bomb parts to supply Ukraine's military.

image.altText: A man in camoflague with his face covered sits between boxes of 3D printed military equipment.

items.title: ASX slips as oil surges back above $US100 on US threat of Gulf blockade

children.content: Failed US-Iran peace talks send oil prices surging higher and the Australian share market lower. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: 3D printed oil barrels and rising stock graph

items.title: Rory McIlroy joins exclusive group with second straight Masters victory

children.content: Rory McIlroy becomes just the fourth man in history to win back-to-back Masters titles after holding off a star-studded field in the final round at Augusta. Follow live.

articles.title: Day rues missed opportunities after golden chance at the Masters

children.content: Australia's Jason Day stays positive after falling to 12th on a difficult final day at the Masters.

image.altText: A man in a white golf shirt holds his hat and looks pensieve after finishing his round.

items.title: Bondi Junction security guard who confronted attacker awarded bravery medal

children.content: Two years on from the Westfield Bondi Junction stabbing attack, eight heroes have been recognised in a special Australian Bravery Decorations honours list.

image.altText: Muhammad Taha sitting on a grey couch wearing a black hoodie.

items.title: Fuel crisis has some delaying retirement and working from home

children.content: The COVID-19 pandemic brought job losses, working from home and economic instability. Some fear the US-Israel war on Iran and spiking fuel prices have created similar struggles.

image.altText: A woman sitting with a guitar looking away from the camera.

items.title: 'Like a flea on an elephant's back': The unexpected rise of solar power

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

items.title: Caretaker files for possession of impressionist Frederick McCubbin's former country home

children.content: A court has heard claims the former home of an Australian impressionist is not safe to walk through, with "black mould, evidence of vermin and wheelie bins collecting rainwater".

image.altText: A two-storey green house with brick chimneys.

items.title: The recycling project turning used tennis balls into shoes

children.content: What should be done with all those used tennis balls that get inevitably sent to landfill? Lilian Xu might have found the answer.

image.altText: Lilian Xu sits with red shoes next to tennis balls in the stands of Kooyng Tennis Club.

items.title: Quiz: Jump in, we're going back in time

children.content: Which state had Australia's only communist MP? And where did the Titanic sink?

image.altText: The RMS Titanic leaves Belfast for sea trials.

items.title: Handwritten church music 'caked in dust' performed after 50 years

children.content: A South Australian church celebrates 150 years with a special performance of sacred music transcribed by early-20th-century parishioners.

image.altText: A man with glasses and a blue coat picking up sheet music from inside a white, rusty chest

items.title: Loved, but costly — is the end near for this heritage railway?

children.content: The West Coast Wilderness Railway in Tasmania is a beloved attraction and an important employer. The state government says it wants the railway to have a sustainable future, but hasn't said how that could be achieved.

items.title: Mystery of seventh man unsolved, months after Chinese boat arrivals

children.content: Months after the arrival of six Chinese nationals in one of Australia's most isolated communities, questions remain about another man suspected of attempting to assist them.

image.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

items.title: Children and 75yo among dozens of homeless in major holiday town

children.content: A homeless survey conducted in one of WA's best-known holiday hotspots uncovers dozens of people sleeping rough, including a 75-year-old.

image.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street

items.title: 'Coddled' and 'dangerously unchallenged': What experts say about Kim Jong Un's daughter

children.content: North Korea leader Kim Jong Un's 13-year-old daughter is tipped to be next in line. So who is Kim Ju Ae?

items.title: 'Missed opportunity': Astronomers lament loss of telescope partnership

children.content: The Artemis II mission used space technology developed in Australia, but experts fear the axing of a multimillion-dollar telescope partnership may stifle innovation and astronomy.

image.altText: Five buildings, four of which are firing lasers from their rooftops, against a starry Milky Way backdrop.

items.title: Domestic violence hotline can only answer 50 per cent of calls

children.content: More than $290 million in federal funding is on the table for domestic violence services nationwide, but Canberra providers are concerned about how it will be distributed and tracked by the ACT government.

image.altText: A person with glasses and a bow tie sits in an office.

items.title: Two people missing after house burns to ground

children.content: A crime scene has been established and two people in their 70s are unaccounted for following a house fire in a remote area of Queensland.

image.altText: Remains of a severely fire damaged home.

items.title: An Aussie rugby win at home is one thing but victory in NZ is another

children.content: Australian teams will only boost their chances of achieving the ultimate success in Super Rugby Pacific if they conquer the challenge of beating their New Zealand opponents across the Tasman.

image.altText: Carter Gordon of the Reds holds the ball as he is tackled by the Crusaders.

items.title: 'Incredible man': Athletes mourn pioneering wheelchair racer Bob Hall

children.content: Bob Hall, who was the first wheelchair athlete to compete in a marathon before becoming a influential race chair designer, dies aged 74.

items.title: North Queensland set for more rain as ex-Cyclone Maila heads for coast

children.content: Beautiful one day, heavy rain, dangerous surf and frosts, the next — Queensland faces a mixed bag of weather throughout the state over the coming days.

image.altText: An elevated view of blue skies over a small city by the beach.

items.title: Tour operator cops $330,000 in cancellations as relief arrives for cyclone-hit businesses

children.content: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference" as Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

items.title: The reality of living without a car in Brisbane

children.content: As the cost of owning a car goes up, some Brisbane residents are choosing to live without one. But going car free isn't possible for everyone.

items.title: Victorian government tried to keep major housing budget blowout under wraps

children.content: Internal documents the Victorian government tried to withhold reveal that a landmark social housing construction project was facing a significant budget blowout two years after it was unveiled.

items.title: 'Help us': Cyclone Maila kills at least 11 people in Papua New Guinea

children.content: The Australian government will give $2.5 million to support relief and recovery efforts in Papua New Guinea and Solomon Islands after Tropical Cyclone Maila.

children.content: How can employers design dog-friendly workplaces if they want to, and what does the law say about animals at work?

items.title: Major setback for beekeepers as varroa mite chemical resistance detected again

children.content: There are growing fears that a second varroa mite strain could enter Australia, as beekeepers lose key chemical options for controlling the tiny parasites of honey bees.
